title: tragia howardii
intro: Tragia howardii, also known as Howard's spurge, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, characterized by its small size and distinctive yellowish-green flowers. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, derived from the presence of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Tragia howardii include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as potential antiviral and antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared by making a tea or infusion from its leaves and roots, which can be used to treat various ailments.
